name: bpftool
title: bpftool
summary: Inspection and simple manipulation of BPF programs and maps
description:
Inspection and simple manipulation of BPF programs and maps
The bpftool command allows for inspection and simple modification of
Berkeley Packet Filter (BPF) objects on the system.

commands.name: bpftool
commands.description: tool for inspection and simple manipulation of eBPF programs and maps

arguments.description: Print all logs available, even debug-level information. This includes logs from libbpf

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bpftool because Inspection and simple manipulation of BPF programs and maps.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use bpftool as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bpftool [OPTIONS] OBJECT { COMMAND | help }` as documented upstream for bpftool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bpftool batch file FILE` as documented upstream for bpftool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bpftool version` as documented upstream for bpftool.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bpftool with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bpftool --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install linux (7.0.12-2kali1). Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `bpftool [OPTIONS] OBJECT { COMMAND | help }`. Inspection and simple manipulation of BPF programs and maps.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Inspection and simple manipulation of BPF programs and maps.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: BPFTOOL - tool for inspection and simple manipulation of eBPF programs and maps.
